On Day 1 of Royal Ascot, Bow Echo showed plenty of grit to narrowly claim the St James's Palace Stakes, while Australian raider Overpass produced a gallant effort to finish third in the Group 1 King Charles III Stakes.

Applications are now open for the Inglis Internship, with former recent graduate Will Gardner highlighting the program as a unique pathway into the thoroughbred industry, offering hands-on experience, invaluable connections and potential long-term career opportunities.

The Jersey Stakes-bound Green Sense topped the Goffs London Sale in Kensington Palace Gardens on Monday evening at £700,000 while Gai Waterhouse and Adrian Bott picked up 3-year-old Santa Bravado alongside McKeever Bloodstock.

Taylor Marshall's breakthrough Group 1 victory aboard Spicy Martini in the Stradbroke Handicap was more than a career-defining success. It was a deeply personal moment, linking father and son through Queensland's greatest race and fulfilling a dream.

Purchased for just $9000 at the Magic Millions March Yearling Sale, Cool Gent has become the ultimate value buy, rewarding the faith of Adam Barling and his ownership group with black-type success and more than $130,000 in prizemoney.

The Australian connection to Royal Ascot grows every year and on day 1, Australians will have Overpass and reigning European Sprinter Of The Year Asfoora to cheer for as they take on the G1 King Charles III Stakes. TTR takes a look at all the Australian influences over the five-day carnival.

Jockey Ryan Moore became the first jockey in history to win every English, Irish and French Classic when Diamond Necklace won Sunday’s G1 Prix de Diane. In Japan, legendary jockey Yutaka Take won his sixth G1 Takarazuka Kinen on Meisho Tabaru who won the race for the second time. Both winners hold nominations for the G1 Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe.
